codeReview.summary: Reviewed feature commit 1c2396bb together with the directly relevant follow-up fixes f4ae033c and cea7a0f4. CLI wording, docs alignment, static-account refresh errors, and runtime-vs-disk refresh targeting are much clearer after those changes, but the TUI footer layout still prevents the new explanatory copy from being visible.

issues.description: The footer block is still allocated `Constraint::Length(3)` even though it now renders four content lines (feedback, selected-account hint, runtime status hint, and key help) inside a bordered paragraph at lines 523-534. In ratatui that height leaves room for only a single inner text row, so the newly added runtime/disk guidance and keybinding help are clipped off-screen. That means the feature does not actually make the cross-surface behavior explicit to operators, and docs/tests now describe footer copy that users cannot see.

sharedStateObservations.observation: TUI copy changes in this mission need a visibility check, not just string-helper tests. The current worker skill and tests verified footer text helpers, but they did not verify that the fixed-height footer block can actually render the added lines.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: rust/proxypilot-rs/src/tui.rs:389 keeps the footer at `Constraint::Length(3)` while rust/proxypilot-rs/src/tui.rs:523-534 renders four lines; the added tests at rust/proxypilot-rs/src/tui.rs:770-842 assert string content only and do not exercise visible layout fit.

summary: Fail: the reviewed feature and its direct follow-up fixes improved CLI/runtime truthfulness, but the TUI footer's fixed height still clips the newly added selected-account/runtime hints and key help, so an operator cannot see the copy intended to explain runtime-vs-disk divergence.
